paper_title: Study Areas and Collection of Samples

methods.description: Process for selecting and collecting samples from various water bodies to study diatom communities and their environmental dependencies.

steps.description: Eight water bodies namely Renuka lake, Macchial lake, Gobind Sagar lake, Rewalsar lake, Manikaran, Pondoh dam, Prashar lake and Nako lake were chosen from seasonal changes and water quality.
steps.searchable_summary: select water bodies based on seasonal changes and water quality

steps.description: Diatom communities exist almost everywhere. The communities that live in water usually consist of more species than those communities found in aerial habitats or in soil.
steps.searchable_summary: identify diatom communities in selected water bodies

steps.description: Each lake or pond can develop a distinctive floral community comprised of specific populations of species.
steps.searchable_summary: document distinctive floral communities in each water body

steps.description: Water bodies with similar physical and chemical characteristics may develop particular algal communities by which they may be distinguished.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze physical and chemical characteristics to distinguish algal communities

steps.description: It is scientifically proven that the growth of diatoms may be highly diversified and specific depending upon seasonal (light and temperature) and other conditions such as salinity, pH and nutrient contents of water.
steps.searchable_summary: study diatom growth under various environmental conditions

steps.description: It was found that at high level altitude, less number of diatoms were found because of the adverse environmental conditions (low temperatures).
steps.searchable_summary: observe diatom presence at high altitudes
